judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly diagnose the root cause, timeline, flakiness misdiagnosis, and the rollback plan's futility, hitting all four expectations equally; Report 2 provides slightly more thorough corroborating evidence for the timeline (git log on the config file itself) but the substance is comparable.

report_1.evidence: config/scoring_config.yaml, which still says expected_label_field: label ... File to change: config/scoring_config.yaml — set expected_label_field: gold_label.

report_2.evidence: File to change to fix it: config/scoring_config.yaml — change expected_label_field: label to expected_label_field: gold_label.

report_1.evidence: It began on 2026-06-17, introduced by commit 53551e8 "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)" (dated 2026-06-17 13:00 UTC). Evidence: pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv shows 0.91 on 2026-06-16 and 0.00 from 2026-06-17 through 2026-07-06 with no recovery

report_2.evidence: pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv shows a clean step: 0.91/0.90/0.92 daily from 2026-05-20 through 2026-06-16, then 0.00 starting 2026-06-17 and every day after. git show 53551e8 shows the commit renamed label→gold_label in all 11 eval records and updated REQUIRED_KEYS in score_batch.py, but its diff does not include config/scoring_config.yaml.

report_1.evidence: score_one.py/score_single() calls only predict_category(normalize_simple(text)) and returns the predicted category — it never loads the eval set, never reads the config's expected_label_field, and never compares against a gold label.

report_2.evidence: score_one.py only runs predict_category(...) and prints the label — it never loads the eval set, never reads expected_label_field, and never performs the prediction == gold comparison.

report_1.evidence: There is no model in the loop to roll back: grep shows model_id is read nowhere in the code, and classification is a deterministic keyword stub. Reverting the "model" changes nothing about the gold-field comparison, so accuracy would remain 0.00 ... The correct next step is the config fix in Q1 plus a regression test; the proposed plan should be dropped.

report_2.evidence: Do not do it — it rests on the same misdiagnosis and will not help. There is no model in this pipeline: the classifier is a deterministic keyword stub, and model_id: ticket-classifier-v3 in the config is dead config read by no code ... "Rolling back the model" is therefore a no-op that cannot move the score; accuracy would stay at 0.00 because the failure is in the eval harness's gold-field lookup, not in the predictions or any model.
